---
title: "Whitetop Sedge (Rhynchospora colorata)"
slug: "rhynchospora-colorata"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474725102/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Whitetop Sedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474725102/medium.jpg" alt="Whitetop Sedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/dbond">
dbond</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/200947415">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=132685)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Common across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A distinctive wetland plant characterized by its grass-like leaves and bright white, leaf-like bracts that surround its flower clusters. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, savannas, and marsh edges. It typically blooms in the warmer months and is especially noticeable when in flower, attracting small pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/132685-Rhynchospora-colorata)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-rhynchospora-colorata/)
